frame_analyses.description: Box trainer setup with 3D Bio foam pad visible, no instruments present yet
Two laparoscopic instruments visible above foam pad, suture material present
Instruments positioned with suture material, preparing for needle loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears to be pre-loaded or being positioned

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument appears to be driving needle through foam tissue
Continuing needle passage through tissue with both instruments engaged
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument coordination for tissue penetration

frame_analyses.description: Needle appears to have completed passage, suture visible on both sides
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bite placement across the tissue

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw formation with instruments manipulating suture ends
Continuing first throw construction with right instrument, appears to be forming surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double wrap visible suggesting surgeon's knot technique

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being constructed with left instrument taking control
Final throw being completed with instruments working in coordination
Knot appears secure and tightened against tissue surface

knot_assessments.notes: Double wrap technique demonstrated for surgeon's knot
Proper hand alternation with single throw
Final securing throw with good technique

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Foam tissue appears well approximated without visible gaps

technique_summary: Instructional demonstration of proper intracorporeal knot tying technique with surgeon's knot and appropriate hand alternation. Good instrument coordination and tissue handling throughout.
improvement_suggestions: This appears to be an instructional video rather than a trainee performance
Focus on maintaining consistent tension during knot tightening
strengths: Excellent demonstration of surgeon's knot technique
Good instrument coordination and economy of motion
